title: The One With The Red Sweater

jokes.text: We did not take a bag of Mashuga Nuts from the minibar. And we did not watch Dr. Do-Me-A-Little.
jokes.setup: Character denying hotel charges to staff
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous names for snacks and porn movie
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay with 'Dr. Do-Me-A-Little' as porn parody name. Economy is excellent - two jokes in one denial.

jokes.text: Joey's immediate 'No' when asked if he was in their room
jokes.setup: Question about whether Joey was in their room
jokes.punchline: Suspiciously quick and defensive denial
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey behavior - immediate denial suggests guilt. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I was told the name of the movie would not appear on the bill.
jokes.setup: Continuing hotel bill dispute
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was assured porn wouldn't show up on bill
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that implies he did watch porn but expected discretion. Builds on earlier joke.

jokes.text: I'll never be a bride again. Now I'm just someone's wife.
jokes.setup: Post-wedding depression
jokes.punchline: Treating 'wife' as a disappointing downgrade from 'bride'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis - obsessing over status changes. Good observational humor about wedding aftermath.

jokes.text: Rapid-fire exchange deflating all of Monica's hopeful suggestions
jokes.setup: Monica listing things to look forward to
jokes.punchline: Each suggestion immediately shot down
jokes.explanation: Well-structured rapid deflation comedy. Good pacing and escalation of disappointment.

jokes.text: I knew I married you for a reason!
jokes.setup: Monica being depressed about marriage
jokes.punchline: Suddenly excited about disposable camera photos
jokes.explanation: Monica's mood swings and obsession with perfection. Good character moment.

jokes.text: So Ross had never checked out of a room a minute before he had to.
jokes.setup: Explanation of Ross's hotel habits
jokes.punchline: Extreme cheapness/rule-following behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross characterization - obsessively maximizing value. Sets up the bigger joke to come.

jokes.text: One time, when we were dating, we got a late checkout. He got so excited, it was the best sex we ever had. Until he screamed out 'Radisson' at the end.
jokes.setup: Story about Ross being excited about late checkout leading to great sex
jokes.punchline: He screamed hotel name during climax
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke construction and character integration. Perfect escalation from innocent setup to shocking punchline. Classic Friends quality.

jokes.text: 32, Joe. You're 32!
jokes.setup: Ross obsessively packing hotel amenities
jokes.punchline: Treating Joey like a child for questioning hotel theft
jokes.explanation: Ross's defensive reaction to being caught hoarding. Decent character moment but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I can't believe Chandler ordered porn on our wedding night.
jokes.setup: Monica seeing hotel bill
jokes.punchline: Shock that Chandler would order porn on wedding night
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke, but fairly predictable reaction. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's sad. Mashuga Nut?
jokes.setup: Monica's distress about porn
jokes.punchline: Joey casually offering the disputed nuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness and timing. Great callback that reveals he did take the nuts.

jokes.text: Thread. Score!
jokes.setup: Ross taking hotel amenities
jokes.punchline: Excitement over finding thread
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross cheapness. One word delivers huge character laugh. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Then you said, 'Why is it called a check? Why not a Yugoslavian?'
jokes.setup: Ross recounting wedding night conversation
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical observation about check names
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that feels authentic to Ross's overthinking personality. Random but character-specific.

jokes.text: Then you did that, and my Uncle Murray looked at me like:
jokes.setup: Ross describing uncle's reaction
jokes.punchline: Ross making an expression of disappointment/confusion
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy moment. Ross's physical comedy skills likely make this work better than it reads.

jokes.text: They're calling out to me. This little guy even crawled up in my lap.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to resist opening wedding presents
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing presents as needy creatures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica compulsiveness. Creates sympathy for inanimate objects, very character-specific.

jokes.text: A little mirror that, when you look into it.. you see yourself as an old woman.
jokes.setup: Guessing what's in a wrapped present
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and dark prediction
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe weirdness - specific, dark, and unexpectedly philosophical. Good character integration.

jokes.text: A tiny salt shaker! Oh, my God, for tiny salt!
jokes.setup: Opening present reveal
jokes.punchline: Disproportionate excitement over mundane item
jokes.explanation: Monica's enthusiasm for domestic items. The 'tiny salt' observation is endearing.

jokes.text: Although, you know, this is part of a salt-and-pepper set. It may just count as only half a present.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to resist opening more presents
jokes.punchline: Rationalizing why opening another is okay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica logic and compulsive behavior. Shows her need to justify her actions.

jokes.text: Or this one!
jokes.setup: Monica rationalizing opening another present
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning logic for impulse
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Monica's complete loss of control. Economic delivery.

jokes.text: He hasn't asked me how I'm feeling or offered to carry my bags. I feel bad for the woman who ends up with him.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complaining about Chandler not treating her like pregnant woman
jokes.punchline: Feeling sorry for his actual wife Monica
jokes.explanation: Good irony since Monica just married him. Phoebe's obliviousness is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: After you, of course.
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizing she just insulted Monica's husband
jokes.punchline: Weak attempt at recovery
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's characteristic foot-in-mouth moment with poor recovery attempt.

jokes.text: Then you have his baby. Believe me, I'm trying.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting Phoebe date tall guy
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing active attempts to get pregnant
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable humor. Phoebe's oversharing is character-appropriate and well-timed.

jokes.text: You're like Ed McMahon, except without the big check. Or the raw sexual magnetism.
jokes.setup: Comparing Rachel telling baby father to Ed McMahon
jokes.punchline: Claiming Ed McMahon had raw sexual magnetism
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and absurd characterization of Ed McMahon. Good structure with escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: He's just walking down the street thinking, 'I had sex with Rachel Greene. I rock!' Then, bam! He's a father, everything's different.
jokes.setup: Describing father's current mindset
jokes.punchline: Dramatic life change visualization
jokes.explanation: Good setup for escalation. Phoebe's dramatic storytelling style shows through.

jokes.text: What was that 'bam'? I don't know. He's hit by a bus, or something.
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning Phoebe's second 'bam'
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting he gets hit by a bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe dark humor delivered casually. The 'or something' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Who called here? Did she sound blond? Did she have an accent? I gotta make a call.
jokes.setup: Joey panicking about pregnancy question
jokes.punchline: Specific criteria for identifying potential baby mama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey promiscuity panic. The 'sound blond' question is especially funny.

jokes.text: Should have never walked into that Sunglass Hut!
jokes.setup: Joey's continued panic about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Blaming a sunglass store for sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist Joey logic. Implies pickup at Sunglass Hut, very specific and character-driven.

jokes.text: Yes, I am. Oh, my God, I'm gonna have a baby!
jokes.setup: Phoebe confirming fake pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Seeming to just realize it herself
jokes.explanation: Phoebe getting caught up in her own lie. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Who's David Linn? Some guy from my gym. Little annoying.
jokes.setup: Joey ready to fight the deadbeat father
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of made-up person
jokes.explanation: 'Little annoying' is a funny understatement for a deadbeat dad. Good improvised lie from Phoebe.

jokes.text: That's why I'm under the table, celebrating.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if he found the cameras
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response while literally under table
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect timing. Physical comedy element adds to the humor.

jokes.text: All my energy is going into not asking that question.
jokes.setup: Ross asking why Monica is depressed after wedding
jokes.punchline: Chandler's survival strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect newlywed humor. Shows Chandler's learning curve about marriage dynamics.

jokes.text: Are you suggesting we dance our troubles away?
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting they use the ballroom
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. Chandler's interpretation is perfectly wrong but logical.

jokes.text: Marriage advice? Really?
jokes.setup: Ross giving advice about not letting Monica down
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Ross's poor relationship track record
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about Ross's multiple divorces. Good economy and character awareness.

jokes.text: I was in charge of the cameras.
jokes.setup: Ross listing all things Monica chose for wedding
jokes.punchline: Chandler's one simple responsibility that he failed
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor. Shows the irony of failing at the simplest task.

jokes.text: Thank God. Because that hair on a baby?
jokes.setup: Learning baby father isn't Gunther
jokes.punchline: Imagining baby with Gunther's distinctive hair
jokes.explanation: Good visual humor referencing Gunther's iconic bleached hair. Easy to picture.

jokes.text: I was walking down the street, thinking, 'I'm gonna tell the father.' And then, bam! Bus?
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her change of mind
jokes.punchline: Phoebe expecting the bus accident
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier bus joke. Phoebe's expectation shows she was listening.

jokes.text: Oh, mother of..!
jokes.setup: Phoebe demonstrating quick Band-Aid removal
jokes.punchline: Extreme pain reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy. The irony of the demonstration backfiring is mildly funny.

jokes.text: Joey, I am out of control! You have to do me a favor. No matter what I say, no matter what I do.. please, do not let me open another present.
jokes.setup: Monica admitting she can't control herself
jokes.punchline: Asking for intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica compulsive behavior. Sets up the immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: Give me one more.
jokes.setup: Monica just asked Joey to stop her from opening presents
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for another present
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction. Excellent economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Phoebe Buffay, will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Joey's speech about scary world for single moms
jokes.punchline: Proposing marriage as solution
jokes.explanation: Sweet and absurd Joey solution. Big moment that escalates well from his concern.

jokes.text: Hey, lady, your day's over. It's my turn.
jokes.setup: Monica objecting to Phoebe marrying Joey
jokes.punchline: Phoebe territorial about wedding attention
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe competitiveness. Treating weddings like shifts is funny.

jokes.text: She's not pregnant. Rachel's the one who's pregnant.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Phoebe's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Revealing Rachel's real pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation. High impact moment that changes everything.

jokes.text: He'd notice when you didn't have a baby in nine months!
jokes.setup: Phoebe insisting she can marry Joey
jokes.punchline: Pointing out obvious flaw in fake pregnancy plan
jokes.explanation: Good logic joke. Monica as the voice of reason in chaotic situation.

jokes.text: Was that story over?
jokes.setup: Long pause after Chandler starts story about the guy
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out the incomplete narrative
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about storytelling. Joey's confusion is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The guy left this. Oh, my God! I know who the father is.
jokes.setup: Showing mysterious item left behind
jokes.punchline: Monica's realization
jokes.explanation: Major plot advancement moment. High impact for story progression.

jokes.text: People have got to finish their stories.
jokes.setup: Another incomplete revelation
jokes.punchline: Joey's frustrated commentary on narrative structure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier frustration. Joey as audience surrogate works well.

jokes.text: Nice aftershave. If you want some, I've got eight mini bottles in my bag.
jokes.setup: Woman complimenting his scent
jokes.punchline: Revealing stolen hotel supplies
jokes.explanation: Good callback to hotel theft subplot. Shows Chandler's guilt and excess.

jokes.text: Your finger was covering the lens. Who are you, Ansel Adams? Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Man refusing to take their picture properly
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive overreaction with photography reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and cultural reference. Chandler's stress showing through anger.

jokes.text: I may play the fool at times.. but I'm more than a pretty blond girl with an ass that won't quit.
jokes.setup: Phoebe defending her intelligence
jokes.punchline: Self-complimentary description while claiming depth
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe self-awareness mixed with vanity. Great character moment that's quotable.

jokes.text: Let's rip. Are you sure? Phoebe! Okay. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Rachel deciding to face the father
jokes.punchline: Phoebe literally ripping something off Rachel
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback to band-aid metaphor. Phoebe's literal interpretation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: A '95 LeSabre? Yes. A green LeSabre? Yes! I'm sorry, I meant a blue LeSabre. Yes, green-blue.
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk trying to get Tag to leave
jokes.punchline: Tag's desperate agreement to contradictory colors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation. 'Green-blue' is a funny compromise attempt.

jokes.text: No, this is my red sweater.
jokes.setup: Phoebe showing him the red sweater as evidence
jokes.punchline: Tag revealing he has an identical sweater
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates the moment. Plot advancement through mistaken identity.

jokes.text: Could I get anyone a coffee? Or poison? No? Just for me? Okay.
jokes.setup: Awkward situation with wrong father
jokes.punchline: Offering poison to herself
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor from Rachel's embarrassment. Good escalation from coffee to poison.

jokes.text: But you do wear that sweater a lot. Are you involved in some kind of dare?
jokes.setup: Trying to make conversation after mistake
jokes.punchline: Insulting his fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor making awkward situation worse. Rachel's nervous chatter.

jokes.text: I'm having a baby.
jokes.setup: Tag asking to get back together
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt revelation that kills the mood
jokes.explanation: Perfect conversation killer. Great economy and timing for maximum awkwardness.

jokes.text: You can go.
jokes.setup: Tag's shocked silence after pregnancy reveal
jokes.punchline: Rachel dismissing him casually
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. Treating the moment like a normal dismissal adds to awkwardness.

jokes.text: I think it does something to salami.
jokes.setup: Looking at strange wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Vague, uncertain description of appliance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about mysterious kitchen gadgets. Relatable wedding gift confusion.

jokes.text: Wow, you didn't even try to unhook my bra.
jokes.setup: Joey hugging Rachel supportively
jokes.punchline: Noting his unusual restraint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character reference. Shows his growth and the seriousness of the moment.

jokes.text: Rachel Greene, will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Joey's speech about supporting single moms
jokes.punchline: Second marriage proposal of the episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey's consistent character. Sweet but absurd logic.

jokes.text: What? I proposed to you. It balances out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Rachel upset about his proposals
jokes.punchline: Joey's mathematical approach to romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic trying to solve emotional complexity with math. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Listen, about that, I only offered.. Ooh, a Salami Buddy!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to take back proposal
jokes.punchline: Phoebe distracted by kitchen gadget
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character moment. Phoebe's easy distraction is very in-character.

jokes.text: Didn't see that coming.
jokes.setup: Monica revealing she found real cameras
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their elaborate deception was pointless
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to plot twist. Chandler's dry delivery works well.

jokes.text: Call it even?
jokes.setup: Monica angry about opened presents, Chandler caught kissing another woman
jokes.punchline: Suggesting their mistakes cancel out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler logic for getting out of trouble. Good relationship humor.

jokes.text: No, the hotel lobby. They think they can charge me for some dirty movie.. and a bag of Mashuga Nuts, they got another thing coming!
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he stole wedding flowers
jokes.punchline: Ross revealing bigger theft motivated by hotel charges
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback. Ross's righteous indignation over false charges drives bigger crime.

jokes.text: Hey, my sweater! I've been looking for this for, like, a month.
jokes.setup: Ross seeing the red sweater evidence
jokes.punchline: Casually claiming ownership
jokes.explanation: Major revelation moment. Perfect casual delivery for such a huge plot point. High impact.

jokes.text: Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Ross claiming the sweater
jokes.punchline: Rachel's realization that Ross is the father
jokes.explanation: Episode climax revelation. Massive impact moment that changes the entire show dynamic.

jokes.text: So whose is it? I don't know, some Italian guy.
jokes.setup: Tag asking about the baby's father
jokes.punchline: Ross unknowingly describing himself
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Ross unknowingly discussing himself as the father. Nice ending beat.

jokes.text: Come on, read your own label. See you later. He is so weird.
jokes.setup: Tag telling Ross to check his sweater label
jokes.punchline: Ross calling Tag weird while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Good ending irony with Ross's obliviousness. Tag's hint goes completely over his head.
